revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23984597483|manual triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:福岡縣護國神社]] {{draft|en}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Fukuokaken Gokoku shrine01.jpg | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | type = ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = |en}} {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture Gokoku Shrine|ja|福岡縣護國神社|1=Fukuoka Gokoku Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka Prefecture Gokoku Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} (福岡縣護國神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Chūō-ku, Fukuoka|en|Chūō-ku, Fukuoka|qid=Q1156273|lt=Chūō-ku, Fukuoka|1=Chūō-ku, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. It is a [[Beppyo Shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!|en}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com|en}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this considering that the shrine is literally named as such?|en}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|en|Yasukuni Shrine|qid=Q242803|lt=Yasukuni Shrine|1=Yasukuni Shrine}}. They were renamed from {{ill|Shōkonsha|en|Shōkonsha|qid=Q11497810|lt=Shokonsha|1=Shōkonsha}} in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X|en}} It enshrines the spirits of approximately 130,000 individuals from Fukuoka Prefecture who perished during conflicts ranging from the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|qid=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration|1=Meiji Restoration}} to the {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|qid=Q184425|lt=Pacific War|1=Pacific War}}. This makes it the second largest Gokoku Shrine in terms of enshrined spirits, after the {{ill|Okinawa Gokoku Shrine|ja|沖縄護国神社|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okinawa Gokoku Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == History == The shrine's origins date back to November {{ill|1868|en|1868|qid=Q7717|lt=1868|1=1868}}, during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Restoration|qq=}}. {{ill|Kuroda Nagatomo|en|Kuroda Nagatomo|qid=Q6446276|lt=Kuroda Nagatomo|1=Kuroda Nagatomo}}, the lord of the Fukuoka domain, established two {{ill|Shōkonsha|ja|招魂社|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkonsha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Shōkonsha|qid=Q11497810}}—one in Nakagōri Kanda (Myōken Shōkonsha) and another in Umaide Village (Umaide Shōkonsha)—to enshrine the spirits of his retainers who died in the {{ill|Boshin War|en|Boshin War|qid=Q309184|lt=Boshin War|1=Boshin War}}. In June {{ill|1906|en|1906|qid=Q2049|lt=1906|1=1906}}, the two were merged into the Myōken Umaide Shōkonsha. The shrine was renamed Fukuoka Shōkonsha in {{ill|1938|en|1938|qid=Q18645|lt=1938|1=1938}} and subsequently rebranded as Fukuoka Gokoku Shrine in {{ill|1939|en|1939|qid=Q18639|lt=1939|1=1939}}, aligning with changes to the national system of Shōkonsha. It became an official prefectural Gokoku Shrine designated by the Ministry of Home Affairs. Four additional Gokoku shrines existed in Fukuoka Prefecture at the time: * '''Yamakawa Gokoku Shrine''' (Yamakawa Village, now {{ill|Kurume|en|Kurume|qid=Q320934|lt=Kurume|1=Kurume}})—established by the Kurume domain. * '''Yanagawa Gokoku Shrine''' (Mitsuhashi Village, now {{ill|Yanagawa, Fukuoka|en|Yanagawa, Fukuoka|qid=Q585630|lt=Yanagawa|1=Yanagawa, Fukuoka}})—established by the Yanagawa domain. * '''Hakkeizan Gokoku Shrine''' (Hakkeizan, Miyako Town)—established by the Kokura domain. * '''Tagawa Gokoku Shrine''' (Ita Town, now {{ill|Tagawa, Fukuoka|en|Tagawa, Fukuoka|qid=Q850332|lt=Tagawa, Fukuoka|1=Tagawa, Fukuoka}})—established by Takachiho nobility. In {{ill|1943|en|1943|qid=Q18623|lt=1943|1=1943}}, the shrine was relocated to its current location near the south side of the Fukuoka Castle ruins, integrating the four other Gokoku shrines and officially becoming '''Fukuoka Prefecture Gokoku Shrine'''. After World War II, it was temporarily renamed '''Shōchūgu''', but it reverted to its original name in {{ill|1957|en|1957|qid=Q5311|lt=1957|1=1957}}. In {{ill|2011|en|2011|qid=Q1994|lt=2011|1=2011}}, the shrine erected a '''Statue of Special Attack Forces Heroes''' to honor fallen kamikaze pilots. == Environment == Situated in central Fukuoka, the shrine shares its grounds with the adjacent {{ill|Ōhori Park|en|Ōhori Park|qid=Q846710|lt=Ōhori Park|1=Ōhori Park}} and {{ill|Maizuru Park (Fukuoka)|ja|舞鶴公園|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maizuru Park (Fukuoka)|1=Jōnai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11613685}}, creating an expansive green space spanning 46,000 hectares. However, issues with stray dogs in the past led to public safety concerns. These have since been resolved through the installation of protective fencing around the grounds. == Festivals == The shrine holds major festivals on: * '''May 3rd & 4th'''—Spring Grand Festival. * '''October'''—Athletics Day and the preceding day. == Access == * Located in Chūō-ku, Fukuoka City, at 1-1-1 Roppommatsu. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * {{ill|Martyrs' shrines (China)|en|Martyrs' shrines (China)|qid=Q6800982|lt=Martyr's Shrines|1=Martyrs' shrines (China)}} * {{ill|Martial temple|en|Martial temple|qid=Q11124210|lt=Martial temple|1=Martial temple}} and {{ill|Wen Wu temple|en|Wen Wu temple|qid=Q7231588|lt=Wen Wu temple|1=Wen Wu temple}} * {{ill|National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|en|National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|qid=Q5956488|lt=National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|1=National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine}} * {{ill|Eternal Spring Shrine|en|Eternal Spring Shrine|qid=Q4501089|lt=Eternal Spring Shrine|1=Eternal Spring Shrine}} * {{ill|Chinese Cultural Renaissance|en|Chinese Cultural Renaissance|qid=Q1073969|lt=Chinese Cultural Renaissance|1=Chinese Cultural Renaissance}} * [[Ancestral shrine]] * {{ill|Gallant Garden|en|Gallant Garden|qid=Q5518867|lt=Gallant Garden|1=Gallant Garden}} * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * {{ill|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|en|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|qid=Q1541043|lt=Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|1=Tomb of the Unknown Soldier}} * {{ill|Arlington National Cemetery|en|Arlington National Cemetery|qid=Q216344|lt=Arlington National Cemetery|1=Arlington National Cemetery}} * {{ill|Valhalla|en|Valhalla|qid=Q177860|lt=Valhalla|1=Valhalla}} (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * {{ill|Walhalla (memorial)|en|Walhalla (memorial)|qid=Q321240|lt=Walhalla Shrine|1=Walhalla (memorial)}} (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * {{ill|Eternal Spring Shrine|en|Eternal Spring Shrine|qid=Q4501089|lt=Eternal Spring Shrine|1=Eternal Spring Shrine}} * {{ill|The common end of myriad good deeds|en|The common end of myriad good deeds|qid=Q9609095|lt=The common end of myriad good deeds|1=The common end of myriad good deeds}} * {{ill|Greek hero cult|en|Greek hero cult|qid=Q1878906|lt=Greek hero cult|1=Greek hero cult}} == Notes == {{Notelist|en}} == External Links == * [http://fukuoka-gokoku.jp/ Official Website] {{Commonscat|Fukuokaken-Gokoku-jinja|en}} == References == {{reflist|en}} |en}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗|en}} {{Source Wikipedia|Fukuoka Gokoku Shrine|en}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \\ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] Gokoku Shrine}} [[ja:福岡縣護國神社]] {{Commons category|Fukuokaken-Gokoku-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11592931}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Chuo-ku, Fukuoka]]
